message.content:
To calculate the average speed for the whole trip, we must use the fundamental definition of average speed:

$$ \text{Average Speed} = \frac{\text{Total Distance}}{\text{Total Time}} $$

**Important Note:** Average speed is **not** the arithmetic mean of the speeds. It depends on the total distance covered divided by the total time elapsed (including any stops).

### Step 1: Calculate the Total Distance

We sum the distances traveled in each leg of the trip.

*   Distance 1 ($d_1$): $120 \text{ km}$
*   Distance 2 ($d_2$): $200 \text{ km}$

$$ \text{Total Distance} = d_1 + d_2 $$
$$ \text{Total Distance} = 120 \text{ km} + 200 \text{ km} = 320 \text{ km} $$

### Step 2: Calculate the Total Time

We must sum the time spent traveling and the time spent stopped. It is crucial to ensure all time units are consistent. We will use **hours**.

*   Time 1 ($t_1$): $1.5 \text{ hours}$
*   Stop Time ($t_{\text{stop}}$): $30 \text{ minutes}$
*   Time 2 ($t_2$): $2 \text{ hours}$

First, convert the stop time from minutes to hours:
$$ 30 \text{ minutes} = \frac{30}{60} \text{ hours} = 0.5 \text{ hours} $$

Now, sum the times:
$$ \text{Total Time} = t_1 + t_{\text{stop}} + t_2
